title: AI first-use and full-game analysis execution implementation

verified_baseline.worktree_and_index: clean before implementation; no untracked files

defects_reproduced.MISSING-UI-009: First-use acceptance was a component-local ref, so closing and reopening the AnalysisPanel in the same SPA session displayed the notice again.
defects_reproduced.MISSING-UI-010: The full-game control was permanently disabled and displayed unsupported copy; no canonical full-game snapshot, progress, cancellation loop, or completed composition existed.
defects_reproduced.NONCONF-UI-017: The analysis surface could execute only a volatile current-node request and therefore could not represent a navigation-stable whole-game task truthfully.
defects_reproduced.COR-001_subset: Current-position output existed, but the first-use and full-game execution subset lacked the task identity and lifecycle required by the corrected audit; candidate insertion and dedicated cancelled/stale surfaces remain deferred.

root_cause_investigation.first_use: AnalysisPanel owned firstUseAccepted as ref(false); destroying the component destroyed the only acceptance owner, while no approved persistence owner was appropriate for session-only consent.
root_cause_investigation.full_game: The Store request context contained one selected node/FEN and its identity acceptance required the focused node to remain current. It had no scope, stable game identity, ordered position set, per-node results, or proven total, so ordinary navigation invalidated the only request shape and a safe whole-game loop could not be built on it.
root_cause_investigation.late_results: The previous acceptance check was current-node-bound and result state was singular. A full-game task required request-level base identity plus per-position immutable identity so cancellation, source changes, and late Worker responses could be rejected without coupling to board focus.
root_cause_investigation.explicit_hypothesis: Moving consent to module-backed Analysis Store session state fixes remount loss without persistence, and replacing the volatile node context with an immutable scoped task snapshot fixes whole-game identity while preserving the stricter node match for current-position analysis.

implementation.surface: WS-001/WS-003 unified local editable workspace analysis region
implementation.scope: MISSING-UI-009, MISSING-UI-010, NONCONF-UI-017, and only the first-use plus full-game execution subset of COR-001
existing_contracts_reused: canonical parsed PGN tree and selected stable game ID
PGN Store source session, source ID, revision, and mutability
existing SearchEngine depth and max-think-time behavior
project Tokens and existing analysis motion tokens

first_use_lifetime.owner: the existing Analysis Store module owns one explicit in-memory application-session acceptance bit
first_use_lifetime.before_acceptance: both current-position and full-game actions show the same ProductConfirmDialog; no SearchEngine or Worker is created before confirmation
first_use_lifetime.cancel: notice cancellation starts no request or Worker, changes no PGN state, and restores focus to the initiating scope control
first_use_lifetime.spa_remount: acceptance survives AnalysisPanel unmount/remount and Store reset/recreation within the loaded application module
first_use_lifetime.full_reload: a full page reload reloads the module and resets acceptance to false
first_use_lifetime.storage: no localStorage, sessionStorage, IndexedDB, URL, Router state, handoff, Settings, Query, or server record is written
first_use_lifetime.browser_storage_evidence: localStorage and sessionStorage remained empty before and after acceptance and current-position completion

full_game_snapshot.ordering: root first, followed by the canonical first-child mainline in deterministic ply order
full_game_snapshot.validation_input_result: the external one-game PGN produced 110 unique frozen positions: initial/root plus 109 canonical mainline plies, ending at 55. Rb7+; the complete parsed tree contained 549 nodes because source variations were retained but not analyzed

task_identity_and_results.current_position: request identity additionally requires the same selected node, FEN, and position hash
task_identity_and_results.full_game: request identity requires the same permission/mutability, source session, source ID, source revision, and stable game ID; ordinary node navigation is intentionally excluded
task_identity_and_results.result_identity: each position result repeats request, scope, source, revision, game, node key, position hash, FEN, label, and ply identity
task_identity_and_results.completed_full_game: the Store publishes one frozen aggregate only after every sequential result is accepted; partial results are not exposed as completed output
task_identity_and_results.retry_and_reanalysis: retry and explicit reanalysis revalidate the current source/game and allocate a fresh request ID plus fresh frozen snapshot
task_identity_and_results.coexisting_result_selection: the panel result animation key and DOM request identity derive from completedScope so current and full-game results cannot borrow each other's request ID

progress_contract.strategy: bounded sequential evaluation through the existing SearchEngine and its existing four-Worker pool
progress_contract.engine_settings: unchanged depth 4 and max-think-time 900 ms per position
progress_contract.completed: the number of accepted position results only
progress_contract.current_position: one-based current index plus stable node ID/key and canonical label
progress_contract.ui: native determinate progress with completed/total/percentage and current-position live text; current-position analysis retains the existing indeterminate presentation

cancellation_and_invalidation.explicit_cancel: clears the active request/progress, stops and terminates the engine pool, prevents another position from starting, and ignores the current late response
cancellation_and_invalidation.cancelled_rerun_evidence: request 2 stopped at 7/110; all four rerun Workers terminated; after a late-result wait the prior completed request 1 still owned all 110 results and request 2 published nothing
cancellation_and_invalidation.source_replacement: opening the same real external PGN as a fresh local source changed source session and source ID, stopped request 2, terminated Workers, cleared full-game matching, and exposed no stale result
cancellation_and_invalidation.source_revision: source revision is part of every request/result base match and the TeachingWorkspace context watcher reconciles it immediately
cancellation_and_invalidation.game_switch: switching from stable game ID 2 to game ID 3 during request 3 stopped the task, terminated Workers, cleared progress, and exposed no matching result
cancellation_and_invalidation.permission_loss: the permission projection is watched and reconcileContext rejects active work when canRunAnalysis becomes false
cancellation_and_invalidation.local_copy_replacement: a new source/session identity cannot match the old task; analysis may start again only as an explicit fresh request on a verified local editable copy
cancellation_and_invalidation.workspace_disposal_and_route_cleanup: source/mode cleanup stops the Store task; Store disposal invalidates the request and destroys the engine

completed_output_and_write_boundary.composition: one read-only summary plus an ordered mainline list of stable label, white-view score, and best move for each matching node
completed_output_and_write_boundary.full_game_browser_result: 110/110 results rendered as 110 list rows with data-analysis-request-id=1 and focus on 重新分析整局
completed_output_and_write_boundary.writeMoveAssessment: retained for the later explicit-action contract, restricted to current-position identity, and has no caller in this slice

source_integrity_evidence.characteristics: one legal Lichess game with a 109-ply mainline and source variations; no file content is embedded in this report
source_integrity_evidence.full_game_projection: before/after PGN plus primitive tree projection SHA-256 remained de3eee20316418305086c27d5e6f5e677373af17c67bc79e06b3584af979a2df; source session, source ID, revision 0, clean revision 0, and game ID remained unchanged; only the user-initiated board navigation changed selectedNodeId
source_integrity_evidence.complete_projection: a separate current-position run normalized headers, tags, PGN text, all 549 mainline/variation nodes and nested fields, annotations/comments, game teaching notes, and draft state into 208,969 bytes; before/after SHA-256 remained a350610ccf2df9e96af83f87997312f624dd9fe5e5feb2fb9af6dedc26320bfe
source_integrity_evidence.unchanged_fields: tree text, source identity, source revision, annotations, comments, teaching notes, variations, selected node, localStorage, and sessionStorage were identical before and after the complete-projection run

accessibility_motion_and_layout.first_use_focus: the safe 暂不开启 action receives initial focus; cancellation returns to the exact 分析当前局面 or 分析整局 trigger
accessibility_motion_and_layout.running_focus: the stable 取消分析 action receives focus after acceptance and reanalysis
accessibility_motion_and_layout.terminal_focus: completion and explicit stop settle focus on the stable 重新分析 or 重新分析整局 action after result motion finishes
accessibility_motion_and_layout.dialog_lifecycle: ProductDialog restores captured/dynamic focus after Naive UI leave cleanup rather than before it
accessibility_motion_and_layout.reduced_motion: current progress remained visible with animation-name none, identity transform, opacity 1, and visibility visible; terminal focus remained correct
accessibility_motion_and_layout.responsive: 1440x960 light, 1280x800 light, 1024x768 light/reduced, and 390x844 dark compositions remained nonblank without document horizontal overflow
accessibility_motion_and_layout.narrow_dialog: the 337px dialog and all actions remained within the 390px viewport and retained one internal overflow owner

readonly_and_contract_blocked_behavior.real_public_data: the real public competition list returned 18,368 items; competition 47347 detail and its read-only venue display rendered real data with zero analysis controls
readonly_and_contract_blocked_behavior.venue_display: the independent read-only display rendered two public pairings, no AnalysisPanel, no analysis request action, and no Vite overlay
readonly_and_contract_blocked_behavior.contract_blocked_replay: /match/dev-slice-008-validation rendered truthful unavailable copy, no request, no AnalysisPanel, and no analysis action
readonly_and_contract_blocked_behavior.competition_commentary_handoff: the selected real pairing produced the truthful contract-unavailable workspace handoff with no canonical PGN source and no analysis controls
readonly_and_contract_blocked_behavior.local_copy_boundary: no synthetic readonly snapshot or local copy was invented; a confirmed local editable copy would be a new source/session and can only run a fresh explicitly accepted task

static_validation.command: mise exec -- pnpm exec vite build --outDir /tmp/chess-pgnviewer-vue-dev-slice-008-build.A0AwJT --emptyOutDir

browser_validation.first_use: fresh current and full-game requests both showed the notice with zero Workers; cancellation started nothing; acceptance created four Workers for the requested scope
browser_validation.lifetime: true AnalysisPanel unmount/remount retained acceptance and the matching result; full reload reset acceptance and Worker count to zero
browser_validation.current_position: one frozen-position request completed with five read-only candidate lines; next-node navigation hid the matching current result while retaining the raw result for its original node
browser_validation.full_game: one immutable 110-position request completed 110 accepted Worker jobs and rendered 110 matching rows; ordinary navigation changed selectedNodeId but retained request ID 1 and monotonic progress
browser_validation.cancellation: a full-game rerun cancelled at 7/110; no later position/result was published, all Workers terminated, and the prior completed request remained unchanged
browser_validation.fresh_identity: explicit full-game reanalysis allocated request ID 2 after request ID 1; a later validated game task allocated request ID 3
browser_validation.source_and_game_invalidation: fresh local source replacement and a real two-game switch each stopped active work and removed matching stale output
browser_validation.source_integrity: the complete normalized PGN projection and storage snapshot were identical before and after analysis
browser_validation.readonly_and_blocked: real competition detail/display and contract-blocked replay/commentary paths exposed no engine action
browser_validation.responsive_and_themes: wide light and narrow dark layouts passed; the narrow consent dialog and analysis actions stayed inside the viewport
browser_validation.reduced_motion: progress remained visible without nonessential animation and completion focus settled correctly
browser_validation.focus: notice cancel, running cancel, completion, explicit stop, and remount focus paths reached stable valid controls
browser_validation.runtime_health: validated fresh reload, integrity, reduced-motion, competition, venue-display, and contract-blocked paths had nonblank DOM, no Vite overlay, no page error, no breaking console error, and no failed response; an established non-breaking favicon 404 appeared only on the first cold dev-server launch
browser_validation.artifacts: no screenshots, browser scripts, fixtures, sample PGNs, logs, or browser artifacts were created in the repository

cor_and_od_impact.MISSING-UI-009: implemented with one memory-only application-session acceptance owner and verified remount/reload/storage boundaries
cor_and_od_impact.MISSING-UI-010: implemented with a canonical immutable full-game task, real determinate progress, cancellation, and minimal read-only completion
cor_and_od_impact.NONCONF-UI-017: implemented for current/full task execution with stable game/node identity and source-safe invalidation
cor_and_od_impact.COR-001: only the first-use and full-game execution subset is implemented; authority-wide closure is not claimed
cor_and_od_impact.OD-03: remains open and unchanged; this slice adds no persistent engine-resource default
cor_and_od_impact.OD-04: remains open and unchanged; this slice adds no persistent analysis preference or retention contract
cor_and_od_impact.OD-01_to_OD-11: all remain visibly open; no provisional value was promoted to authority

remaining_dev_slice_009_work: dedicated stale-result product surface and terminology cleanup
explicit candidate-line insertion and its write boundary
any approved explicit assessment/variation write action
remaining_cor_001_work: DEV-SLICE-009 candidate insertion and cancelled/stale presentation
owner decisions for any persistent engine resource/default/retention behavior
future contract-backed remote/read-only copy analysis only after a real canonical source contract exists
limitations: Full-game execution is intentionally sequential and can take several minutes for a long source game at the existing depth and think-time settings.
Cancelling a rerun preserves a still-valid earlier completed result; DEV-SLICE-009 owns any dedicated cancelled-state product treatment.
No confirmed readonly pairing PGN contract existed in the real competition handoff, so no synthetic readonly snapshot or editable-copy transition was fabricated.
The development server has an established non-breaking missing favicon resource message on its first cold launch; all fresh validation routes after reload were console-clean.

review.result: independent final diff review found one scope-identity issue, it was fixed, and no additional finding remained
review.addressed: resultAnimationKey and data-analysis-request-id now derive from completedScope so coexisting current/full results cannot expose or animate under the wrong request identity

commit_subject: feat(analysis): 完成首次提示与整局分析执行闭环
